task_completion.description: Access the 'Introduction to Biology' (BIO101) course in Moodle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the Moodle interface navigated to the 'Introduction to Biology' course page, typically with editing mode turned on.

task_completion.description: Add a new forum named 'Clinical Case Study Discussions' with the required description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Moodle activity creation page showing 'Clinical Case Study Discussions' in the Forum name field and the text starting with 'Weekly forum for analyzing clinical case studies...' in the Description field.

task_completion.description: Set the forum type to 'Standard forum for general use' and subscription mode to 'Forced subscription'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the forum settings showing 'Standard forum for general use' selected in the Forum type dropdown, and 'Forced subscription' selected in the dropdown under the 'Subscription and tracking' section.

task_completion.description: Draft a new discussion topic with the subject 'Week 1: Cellular Respiration Disorder' and the required clinical case message.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the 'Add a new discussion topic' form showing the Subject 'Week 1: Cellular Respiration Disorder' and the Message box containing the clinical case study details ('A 45-year-old patient presents with extreme fatigue...').

task_completion.description: Successfully post the discussion topic to the forum.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the 'Clinical Case Study Discussions' forum page showing the 'Week 1: Cellular Respiration Disorder' topic listed in the discussions table, or a green success banner indicating the post was successfully added.

integrity.description: Agent used the Moodle web interface to create the forum and post the discussion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating Moodle, clicking 'Turn editing on', using the activity chooser, and filling out web forms, rather than executing terminal scripts or database commands.

integrity.description: Agent entered the text into the actual Moodle text editors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the text being typed or pasted into Moodle's rich text editor (Atto/TinyMCE) fields for the description and message, rather than modifying the DOM directly via developer tools.

integrity.description: Agent navigated through the course to add the activity.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Moodle navigation steps, such as viewing the course page, clicking 'Add an activity or resource', and selecting 'Forum'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Course Shortname: BIO101. Expected Forum Name: 'Clinical Case Study Discussions'. Expected Forum Type: 'Standard forum for general use' (internally represented as 'general'). Expected Subscription Mode: 'Forced subscription' (internally represented as value 1). Expected Topic Subject: 'Week 1: Cellular Respiration Disorder'. The message must contain clinical terms including 'fatigue' and 'mitochondrial'.
